title: Collapse Subitems in Gantt
excerpt:
Would love to be able to collapse subitems in the Gantt chart view. Currently, only the Group can be collapsed and expanded in the Gantt view.

posts.author: cberman (Corey)
posts.cookedHtml:
Would love to be able to collapse subitems in the Gantt chart view. Currently, only the Group can be collapsed and expanded in the Gantt view.

My use case is a high-level board where each pulse is a project, and the subitems are milestones of the project. With subitems collapsed, Gantt is a good overview of many projects on one timeline. Then it would be great to just expand milestones for the project of interest. Currently, I have a timeline for the projects and the dates for the milestones enabled for the Gantt view. It works well to show the milestones cascaded under the project, but it takes up a lot of vertical space because nothing can collapse.

posts.author: pjimenez (Pedro Jimenez)
posts.cookedHtml: I agree, it’s currently all or nothing.

posts.author: BernardVC (Bernard  Van Cauwenberghe)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello,

It is easy solved with an extra column
1/ Add column “Subitem Collapsed”
2/ Then give ever subitem the label “Collapsed”
3/ Then put a filter on the gantt
4/ Problem is solved.

Not perfect, but it works.

Kind regards,

Bernard

posts.author: nboeke (Nicholas Boeke)
posts.cookedHtml: You can change the Gantt view to hide subitems; it’s under the gantt settings. If you uncheck the Subitem columns timeline, then only the Project level items will appear in your gannt chart.

posts.author: lnorris25 (Liam)
posts.cookedHtml: What if I want to show some subitems of a parent, but not all of them?

posts.author: rosalind (Rosalind Guy)
posts.cookedHtml: Similar to the answer from BernardVC, add a sub item column e.g. status = Gantt or Collapsed and then use the filters to include or exclude different status. Totally agree it is not perfect.
